chapters.title: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration
chapters.description: Modern periodic table, electronic configuration, blocks, groups, periods and periodic trends.

lessons.title: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Overview
lessons.title_ml: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Overview

test.title: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Overview - Test

questions.text: Which feature mainly decides the period of an element?
questions.explanation: The period number corresponds to the number of occupied electron shells.

questions.text: Elements in the same group usually have similar chemical properties because they have the same:
questions.explanation: Valence electrons control most chemical properties, so group members behave similarly.

questions.text: The maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in the second shell is:
questions.explanation: By the 2n^2 rule, the second shell can hold 8 electrons.

test.title: Electronic Configuration and Shells - Test

questions.text: Elements in the same group usually have similar chemical properties because they have the same:
questions.explanation: Valence electrons control most chemical properties, so group members behave similarly.

questions.text: The maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in the second shell is:
questions.explanation: By the 2n^2 rule, the second shell can hold 8 electrons.

questions.text: An element ending in p subshell belongs to which block?
questions.explanation: The block is named from the subshell into which the last electron enters.

test.title: Groups, Periods and Blocks - Test

questions.text: The maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in the second shell is:
questions.explanation: By the 2n^2 rule, the second shell can hold 8 electrons.

questions.text: An element ending in p subshell belongs to which block?
questions.explanation: The block is named from the subshell into which the last electron enters.

questions.text: Across a period from left to right, atomic size generally:
questions.explanation: Effective nuclear charge increases across a period, pulling electrons closer.

test.title: Periodic Trends - Test

questions.text: An element ending in p subshell belongs to which block?
questions.explanation: The block is named from the subshell into which the last electron enters.

questions.text: Across a period from left to right, atomic size generally:
questions.explanation: Effective nuclear charge increases across a period, pulling electrons closer.

questions.text: Which element has electronic configuration 2,8,1?
questions.explanation: Sodium has atomic number 11 and configuration 2,8,1.

lessons.title: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Revision
lessons.title_ml: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Revision

test.title: Periodic Table and Electronic Configuration - Revision - Test

questions.text: Across a period from left to right, atomic size generally:
questions.explanation: Effective nuclear charge increases across a period, pulling electrons closer.

questions.text: Which element has electronic configuration 2,8,1?
questions.explanation: Sodium has atomic number 11 and configuration 2,8,1.

questions.text: The modern periodic table is arranged mainly according to increasing:
questions.explanation: Modern periodic law is based on atomic number.

chapters.title: Gas Laws and Mole Concept
chapters.description: Gas laws, pressure-volume-temperature relations, Avogadro's law, mole concept and molar volume.

lessons.title: Gas Laws and Mole Concept - Overview
lessons.title_ml: Gas Laws and Mole Concept - Overview

test.title: Gas Laws and Mole Concept - Overview - Test

questions.text: At constant temperature, the volume of a fixed mass of gas is inversely proportional to its:
questions.explanation: Boyle's law states that V is inversely proportional to P at constant temperature.

questions.text: Charles's law relates the volume of a gas to its:
questions.explanation: At constant pressure, gas volume is directly proportional to absolute temperature.

questions.text: One mole of any substance contains:
questions.explanation: One mole contains Avogadro's number of particles.

test.title: Boyle's Law and Charles's Law - Test

questions.text: Charles's law relates the volume of a gas to its:
questions.explanation: At constant pressure, gas volume is directly proportional to absolute temperature.

questions.text: One mole of any substance contains:
questions.explanation: One mole contains Avogadro's number of particles.

questions.text: The molar volume of an ideal gas at STP is approximately:
questions.explanation: One mole of an ideal gas occupies about 22.4 L at STP.

test.title: Avogadro's Law and Molar Volume - Test

questions.text: One mole of any substance contains:
questions.explanation: One mole contains Avogadro's number of particles.

questions.text: The molar volume of an ideal gas at STP is approximately:
questions.explanation: One mole of an ideal gas occupies about 22.4 L at STP.

questions.text: The SI unit commonly used for amount of substance is:
questions.explanation: Amount of substance is measured in mole.

lessons.title: Mole, Molar Mass and Number of Particles
lessons.title_ml: Mole, Molar Mass and Number of Particles

test.title: Mole, Molar Mass and Number of Particles - Test

questions.text: The molar volume of an ideal gas at STP is approximately:
questions.explanation: One mole of an ideal gas occupies about 22.4 L at STP.

questions.text: The SI unit commonly used for amount of substance is:
questions.explanation: Amount of substance is measured in mole.

questions.text: If pressure on a gas is increased at constant temperature, its volume:
questions.explanation: Boyle's law predicts reduced volume when pressure increases.

questions.explanation: Boyle's law predicts reduced volume when pressure increases.

questions.explanation: CO2 has mass 12 + 2 x 16 = 44 g/mol.

questions.text: Charles's law relates the volume of a gas to its:

questions.text: If pressure on a gas is increased at constant temperature, its volume:
questions.explanation: Boyle's law predicts reduced volume when pressure increases.

questions.text: Molar mass of carbon dioxide, CO2, is:
questions.explanation: CO2 has mass 12 + 2 x 16 = 44 g/mol.

questions.text: Equal volumes of gases at the same temperature and pressure contain equal numbers of molecules. This is:
questions.explanation: Avogadro's law connects gas volume and number of molecules.

chapters.title: Reactivity Series and Electrochemistry
chapters.description: Reactivity series, displacement reactions, galvanic cells, electrolysis and electrochemical applications.

test.title: Reactivity Series and Electrochemistry - Overview - Test

questions.text: Which metal is more reactive than copper?
questions.explanation: Zinc is above copper in the reactivity series and can displace copper from its salts.

questions.text: A metal higher in the reactivity series can displace a metal lower from its:
questions.explanation: Displacement reactions occur when a more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal from a salt solution.

questions.text: In a galvanic cell, chemical energy is converted into:
questions.explanation: Galvanic cells produce electrical energy from spontaneous chemical reactions.

test.title: Reactivity Series and Displacement - Test

questions.text: A metal higher in the reactivity series can displace a metal lower from its:
questions.explanation: Displacement reactions occur when a more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal from a salt solution.

questions.text: In a galvanic cell, chemical energy is converted into:
questions.explanation: Galvanic cells produce electrical energy from spontaneous chemical reactions.

questions.text: The electrode at which oxidation occurs is called the:
questions.explanation: Oxidation occurs at the anode.

test.title: Galvanic Cells and Electrodes - Test

questions.text: In a galvanic cell, chemical energy is converted into:
questions.explanation: Galvanic cells produce electrical energy from spontaneous chemical reactions.

questions.text: The electrode at which oxidation occurs is called the:
questions.explanation: Oxidation occurs at the anode.

questions.text: Electrolysis is carried out using:
questions.explanation: Electrolysis uses direct current to drive a non-spontaneous chemical reaction.

test.title: Electrolysis and Electroplating - Test

questions.text: The electrode at which oxidation occurs is called the:
questions.explanation: Oxidation occurs at the anode.

questions.text: Electrolysis is carried out using:
questions.explanation: Electrolysis uses direct current to drive a non-spontaneous chemical reaction.

questions.text: Electroplating is used mainly to:
questions.explanation: Electroplating deposits a thin layer of one metal on another object.

test.title: Reactivity Series and Electrochemistry - Revision - Test

questions.text: Electrolysis is carried out using:
questions.explanation: Electrolysis uses direct current to drive a non-spontaneous chemical reaction.

questions.text: Electroplating is used mainly to:
questions.explanation: Electroplating deposits a thin layer of one metal on another object.

questions.text: Which ion gains electrons during reduction?
questions.explanation: Positive ions commonly gain electrons at the cathode during reduction.

questions.text: Electrolysis is carried out using:
questions.explanation: Electrolysis uses direct current to drive a non-spontaneous chemical reaction.

questions.text: Electroplating is used mainly to:
questions.explanation: Electroplating deposits a thin layer of one metal on another object.

questions.text: Which ion gains electrons during reduction?
questions.explanation: Positive ions commonly gain electrons at the cathode during reduction.

questions.text: The salt bridge in a cell helps to:
questions.explanation: A salt bridge allows ion movement and maintains charge balance.

chapters.title: Production of Metals
chapters.description: Occurrence of metals, concentration of ores, extraction, refining and corrosion prevention.

test.title: Production of Metals - Overview - Test

questions.text: A naturally occurring substance from which a metal can be economically extracted is called an:
questions.explanation: An ore is a mineral deposit from which metal extraction is economical.

questions.text: Highly reactive metals such as sodium are commonly extracted by:
questions.explanation: Highly reactive metals need electrolytic extraction from molten compounds.

questions.text: The process of removing impurities from ore before extraction is:
questions.explanation: Ore concentration enriches useful mineral content by removing gangue.

test.title: Minerals, Ores and Concentration - Test

questions.text: Highly reactive metals such as sodium are commonly extracted by:
questions.explanation: Highly reactive metals need electrolytic extraction from molten compounds.

questions.text: The process of removing impurities from ore before extraction is:
questions.explanation: Ore concentration enriches useful mineral content by removing gangue.

questions.text: The main ore of aluminium is:
questions.explanation: Bauxite is the chief ore of aluminium.

test.title: Extraction by Reduction and Electrolysis - Test

questions.text: The process of removing impurities from ore before extraction is:
questions.explanation: Ore concentration enriches useful mineral content by removing gangue.

questions.text: The main ore of aluminium is:
questions.explanation: Bauxite is the chief ore of aluminium.

questions.text: Corrosion of iron is commonly known as:
questions.explanation: Rusting is the corrosion of iron in the presence of oxygen and moisture.

test.title: Refining, Alloys and Corrosion - Test

questions.text: The main ore of aluminium is:
questions.explanation: Bauxite is the chief ore of aluminium.

questions.text: Corrosion of iron is commonly known as:
questions.explanation: Rusting is the corrosion of iron in the presence of oxygen and moisture.

questions.text: Coating iron with zinc to prevent rusting is called:
questions.explanation: Galvanisation protects iron by a zinc coating.

test.title: Production of Metals - Revision - Test

questions.text: Corrosion of iron is commonly known as:
questions.explanation: Rusting is the corrosion of iron in the presence of oxygen and moisture.

questions.text: Coating iron with zinc to prevent rusting is called:
questions.explanation: Galvanisation protects iron by a zinc coating.

questions.text: An alloy is best described as:
options: A mixture of metals or a metal with another element
questions.explanation: Alloys are mixtures designed to improve useful properties.

questions.explanation: Rusting is the corrosion of iron in the presence of oxygen and moisture.

questions.text: Coating iron with zinc to prevent rusting is called:
questions.explanation: Galvanisation protects iron by a zinc coating.

questions.text: An alloy is best described as:
options: A mixture of metals or a metal with another element
questions.explanation: Alloys are mixtures designed to improve useful properties.

questions.text: Electrolytic refining uses an impure metal as the:
questions.explanation: In electrolytic refining, impure metal is made the anode and pure metal deposits on the cathode.

chapters.title: Compounds of Non-Metals

chapters.title: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism
chapters.description: Organic compounds, functional groups, IUPAC nomenclature, homologous series and structural isomerism.

lessons.title: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism - Overview
lessons.title_ml: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism - Overview

test.title: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism - Overview - Test

questions.text: The simplest alkane is:
questions.explanation: Methane, CH4, is the first member of the alkane series.

questions.text: The general formula of alkanes is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with formula CnH2n+2.

questions.text: The functional group of alcohols is:
questions.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group, -OH.

test.title: Hydrocarbons and Homologous Series - Test

questions.text: The general formula of alkanes is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with formula CnH2n+2.

questions.text: The functional group of alcohols is:
questions.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group, -OH.

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulae are called:
questions.explanation: Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but different atom arrangements.

test.title: Functional Groups and IUPAC Names - Test

questions.text: The functional group of alcohols is:
questions.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group, -OH.

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulae are called:
questions.explanation: Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but different atom arrangements.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of C2H6 is:
questions.explanation: A two-carbon saturated hydrocarbon is named ethane.

test.title: Structural Isomerism - Test

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulae are called:
questions.explanation: Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but different atom arrangements.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of C2H6 is:
questions.explanation: A two-carbon saturated hydrocarbon is named ethane.

questions.text: A carbon-carbon double bond is found in:
questions.explanation: Alkenes contain at least one C=C bond.

test.title: Nomenclature and Isomerism - Revision - Test

questions.text: The IUPAC name of C2H6 is:
questions.explanation: A two-carbon saturated hydrocarbon is named ethane.

questions.text: A carbon-carbon double bond is found in:
questions.explanation: Alkenes contain at least one C=C bond.

questions.text: The prefix 'prop-' in organic nomenclature indicates:
questions.explanation: The root prop- denotes a chain of three carbon atoms.

chapters.title_ml: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism

test.title: Nomenclature of Organic Compounds and Isomerism - Chapter Test

questions.text: The simplest alkane is:
questions.explanation: Methane, CH4, is the first member of the alkane series.

questions.text: The general formula of alkanes is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with formula CnH2n+2.

questions.text: The functional group of alcohols is:
questions.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group, -OH.

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulae are called:
questions.explanation: Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but different atom arrangements.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of C2H6 is:
questions.explanation: A two-carbon saturated hydrocarbon is named ethane.

questions.text: A carbon-carbon double bond is found in:
questions.explanation: Alkenes contain at least one C=C bond.

questions.text: The prefix 'prop-' in organic nomenclature indicates:
questions.explanation: The root prop- denotes a chain of three carbon atoms.

questions.text: Members of a homologous series differ by:
questions.explanation: Successive members of a homologous series usually differ by a CH2 group.

chapters.title: Chemical Reactions of Organic Compounds
chapters.description: Combustion, substitution, addition, oxidation, polymerisation and reactions of common organic compounds.

lessons.title: Chemical Reactions of Organic Compounds - Overview
lessons.title_ml: Chemical Reactions of Organic Compounds - Overview

test.title: Chemical Reactions of Organic Compounds - Overview - Test

questions.text: Complete combustion of hydrocarbons mainly produces carbon dioxide and:
questions.explanation: Complete combustion of hydrocarbons forms carbon dioxide and water.

questions.text: A reaction in which hydrogen atoms in methane are replaced by chlorine is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes undergo substitution reactions with chlorine in sunlight.

questions.text: Unsaturated hydrocarbons commonly undergo:
questions.explanation: Double or triple bonds allow addition reactions.

test.title: Combustion and Substitution Reactions - Test

questions.text: A reaction in which hydrogen atoms in methane are replaced by chlorine is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes undergo substitution reactions with chlorine in sunlight.

questions.text: Unsaturated hydrocarbons commonly undergo:
questions.explanation: Double or triple bonds allow addition reactions.

questions.text: Polyethene is formed by polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Ethene monomers join to form polyethene.

questions.text: Unsaturated hydrocarbons commonly undergo:
questions.explanation: Double or triple bonds allow addition reactions.

questions.text: Polyethene is formed by polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Ethene monomers join to form polyethene.

questions.text: Oxidation of ethanol can produce:
questions.explanation: Ethanol can be oxidised to ethanoic acid under suitable conditions.

test.title: Oxidation and Reactions of Alcohols - Test

questions.text: Polyethene is formed by polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Ethene monomers join to form polyethene.

questions.text: Oxidation of ethanol can produce:

questions.text: The presence of a double bond can be tested using decolourisation of:
questions.explanation: Unsaturated compounds decolourise bromine water by addition.
